intentional_test_features: Over-triage / over-treatment resistance: an explosive, weeping, crusting facial and eyelid flare with intense burning and severe psychosocial distress is an alarming red herring pulling toward emergency referral and antibiotics. The correct read is a benign, common, chronic-relapsing greasy-scale facial/scalp eruption with associated anterior lid-margin crusting, telehealth-manageable with an antifungal shampoo, a short course of mild topical steroid and lid hygiene, plus a prominent eye safety-net - NOT ED escalation or systemic antibiotics when the sight-threat red-flag screen is negative.

Periocular red-herring mimic: the telephone-triage framing explicitly raised ruling out periorbital cellulitis, herpetic keratitis and eczema herpeticum. On the patient-obtainable data the sight-threat screen is NEGATIVE (no true deep eye pain, no photophobia, no vision change, no fever, no spreading hot periorbital swelling, no grouped vesicles/punched-out erosions). The reasoning task is to recognise the benign cause and attach a mandatory eye safety-net rather than reflexively escalating because the eye cannot be examined remotely.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is an in-person out-of-hours residential visit with a full clinician examination (close periocular inspection, conjunctival assessment, extraocular movements, visual-acuity screen, measured vitals). Clinician-only findings (mildly injected conjunctivae, intact EOM, grossly normal acuity, absence of vesicles/punched-out erosions/spreading cellulitic change, measured vitals) are sealed in 10/11 telehealth_limits; patient/video-observable data (greasy scale distribution, eyelid crusting/stuck-shut eyes, burning) are placed in 01.

Demographics inferred: age not stated in the source (adult inferred from employment, chronic dandruff history and 78 kg body weight); sex not stated and assigned solely for schema conformance - both flagged for clinician confirmation.
Source category: DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue), source note DST-036.
